title: Kino no Tabi -the Beautiful World- Haikyo no Kuni
titleRomaji: Kino no Tabi -the Beautiful World- Haikyo no Kuni
titleNative: キノの旅 -the Beautiful World- 廃墟の国
synonyms: Kino no Tabi: The Beautiful World - The Animated Series - Haikyo no Kuni - On Your Way
synopsis: It is a tasuketsu (multiple choice) drama split into 3 separate stories featuring the three primary trios: Kino/Hermes, Shizu/Ti/Riku and Shishou/Aibou with multiple options to choose from throughout their stories, however, all three focus on the travelers of choice venturing into the Country of Ruins and meeting the lone citizen Sera.

title: Blade
synonyms: Blade – Wieczny łowca
synopsis: The story revolves around Blade, a "Daywalker" vampire hunter who was born with vampire and human blood after a vampire attacked his mother. While visiting Japan on a mission, Blade tracks down Deacon Frost, the vampire who killed his mother. Blade confronts "Existence," a mysterious organization composed of vampires modified by Frost. (Source: Anime News Network)

title: Sci-fi Harry
synopsis: Harry is definitely not your average American teenager. Instead, he is the epitome of an "alienated youth" - friendless at school, extremely weird and nervous to the point of paranoia. But a chance occurrence causes Harry to start to demonstrate what appear to be psychic powers - yet he neither believes in them nor consciously controls them. But there are other ominous forces at work who do believe in Harry, and attempt to make use of him in ways that are threatening and frightful. (Source: AniDB)

title: 18if
synopsis: Haruto Tsukishiro wakes up in a dream world and sees a mysterious girl named Lily. Following this, he meets a man named Kanzaki and learns of the existence of witches, which from here on entangles him in all sorts of incidents. 18if is a part of a multi-media project that also includes a mobile game and a virtual reality game.

title: The Price of Smiles
synonyms: The Cost of Smiles
synopsis: Original animation celebrating Tatsunoko Production's 55th anniversary. On a planet far from Earth, there is a kingdom full of smiling faces. Princess Yuuki is 12 years old, and about to enter a sensitive age in a person's life. Everyday, she cries, laughs, and sometimes, her heart throbs with excitement. All the while, she lives merrily in the royal palace. Filling her days with color are her loyal vassals: her tutor Reira, Izana who assists in political affairs, the leader of the chivalry Harold … and then, there is her childhood friend and aide Joshua. "Yuuki! If you have spirit and guts, you can do anything!" "…No, not this again! Joshua, be nobler!" Stella is 17 years old and a capable, reserved soldier. However, she is always smiling ... for smiling is essential to living. This is a story of two girls born on distant planets. (Source: Tatsunoko)

title: Breakage
synonyms: Break-age
synopsis: In 2007, the inhabitants of Danger Planet II fight their battles using remote-controlled robots also known as virtual puppets. Kirio Nimura is a high school student and the strongest puppeteer on the planet who falls for Sairi Takahara; however, she insists that nobody may make advances until they have beaten her puppet, Benkei. (Source: Anime Encyclopedia)

title: Ganbare! Kakure Ase Project
titleNative: がんばれ！隠れ汗 プロジェクト
synonyms: Biore Commercial
Bioré CM
synopsis: An animated commercial directed by Yoshihiro Kanno for Kao Corporation. The theme song track is "I can" by Sayaka Inoue. Promoting Kao Bioré Sarasara Powder Sheets, the ad stars three high school friends, one a flutist named Mina and the other a burgeoning photographer named Akane. Mina finds herself getting wistful as the school year is drawing to a close but through the encouragement of her friends, she's reinvigorated to make the year the best it can be. (Source: Anime News Network, edited)

title: NAGASAKI 1945: Angelus no Kane
synonyms: The Bells of Nagasaki
Nagasaki 1945 ~ The Angelus Bells
synopsis: August 1945. In a converted theological seminary on the outskirts of Nagasaki, a young doctor works to help his patients, as unsettling news comes of a new weapon used by the Americans on the city of Hiroshima. When the second nuclear weapon is dropped on Nagasaki, the hospital staff must work to save lives in the midst of the concentric circles of death that their city has become.

title: Animation × Paralympic: Who Is Your Hero?
titleRomaji: ANI x PARA: Anata no Hero wa Dare desu ka
titleEnglish: Animation × Paralympic: Who Is Your Hero?
titleNative: アニ×パラ〜あなたのヒーローは誰ですか〜
synopsis: “ANIMATION × PARALYMPIC: Who Is Your Hero?” is a special series featuring fictional Paralympic athletes depicted in world-renowned Japanese animation. The project aims to introduce new Paralympic sports heroes through attractive characters and stories that will be created by the collaboration of famous comic creators and animation film directors. The series will increase the number of featured sports by 2020. An episode animated by Kyoto Animation was scheduled to be broadcast in August 2019, but was later announced to be cancelled after it was determined production could not be completed in time for the 2020 Paralympic Games due to a tragic arson attack on the studio.

synopsis: Hello Kitty and other Sanrio characters star in adaptations of various fairy tales from all over the world. This entry contains: 1. Hello kitty no Shirayukihime / Hello Kitty in "Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs" (ハローキティの白雪姫) 2. Bad Badtz-maru no Ookami ga Kita! / Badtz-Maru in "The Shepherd Boy and the Wolf" (バッドばつ丸のオオカミが来た！) 3. Pochakko no Jack to Mame no ki / Pochacco in "Jack and the Beanstalk" (ポチャッコのジャックと豆の木) 4. Hello Kitty no Susama no Mimi wa Roba no Mimi / Hello Kitty & Mimmy in "The Story of King Midas" (ハローキティの王様の耳はロバの耳) 5. Hello Kitty no Hansel to Gretel / Hello Kitty & Dear Daniel in "Hansel & Gretel" (ハローキティのヘンゼルとグレーテル) 6. My Melody no Akazukin / My Melody in "Little Red Riding Hood" (マイメロディの赤ずきん) 7. Hello Kitty no Kaguya-Hime / Hello Kitty in "The Bamboo Princess" (ハローキティのかぐや姫) 8. Hangyodon no Hadaka no Ousama / Hangyodon in "The Emperor's New Clothes" (ハンギョドンのはだかの王様) 9. Hello Kitty no Nemureru Mori no Bijo / Hello Kitty in "Sleeping Beauty" (ハローキティのねむれる森の美女) 10. Bad Badt

synopsis: Young Sanshiro Kurenai is the proud heir of a judo dojo. One day, he finds his beloved father dead and a glass eye not too far from the corpse. Blue: Line Step Brush
synopsis: Promotional video for Amgakuin (AMG), a school for artists who want to become active in the entertainment industry. This PV is directed by Kengo Saitou and produced for the institute's 25th anniversary. All key figures to this animation are alumni from AMG and are now successfully working in the anime industry. The theme song is "Ikiru" by the band UNDER GRAPH.

title: Twin Angels BREAK
synopsis: When the original team goes missing, a mysterious hedgehog named Miruku-chan requests two 3rd year middle schoolers to become the new Twin Angels. The cheerful yet forceful Meguru Amatsuki innocently aspires to be a hero of justice. The cool-headed introvert Sumire Kisaragi grew up in a strict household. Despite their differences, they start opening up to each other as they work as Twin Angels to stop the villainous Mary from resurrecting the demon lord Zelucifer. (Source: Edited from CR)

title: Midori-ko
synopsis: One of the must-see gems making its premiere at our festival, Midori-Ko is adored Japanese animator Kurosaka Keita's whimsically nightmarish vision of 21st-century Tokyo on the brink of apocalypse. Ten years in the making and entirely, single-handedly rendered in colored pencil, Kurosaka's fantastical labor of love is a marvel to behold. Emerging from the staggering detail and craft flooding every frame is the story of a young woman who sets out to engineer a dream-food that can put an end to the world's famine. Synthesizing Frederic Back's subtle, haptic textures with Bill Plympton's frenetic mutations and David Lynch's haunting wormholes, Kurosaka’s work still retains its own singular, luminous potency. (Source: Los Angeles Animation Festival summary)

tags.name: Food
tags.description: Centers around cooking or food appraisal.

tags.name: Dystopian
tags.description: Partly or completely set in a society characterized by poverty, squalor or oppression.

tags.name: Cultivation
tags.description: Features characters using training, often martial arts-related, and other special methods to cultivate qi (a component of traditional Chinese philosophy, described as "life force") and gain strength or immortality.

tags.name: Nudity
tags.description: Features a character wearing no clothing or exposing intimate body parts.

tags.name: Surreal Comedy
tags.description: Prominently features comedic moments that defy casual reasoning, resulting in illogical events.
